Article.ArticleTitle: Experimental Analysis of the Correlation Between Cutting Parameters and Recurrence Measures During End Mill Flute Grinding.

Abstract.AbstractText: This paper presents a comprehensive analysis of the flute grinding process in end cutters, focusing on the influence of machining parameters on recurrence indicators. Recurrence quantification analysis (RQA) was employed to assess the dynamic behavior, regularity, and predictability of the process. Based on experimental data, a grinding force model was developed, along with mathematical formulations of the recurrence indicators. Leveraging these models, a novel parameter, the dominant index was proposed to identify which cutting parameters have the greatest impact on the RQA measures. The results reveal that cutting speed exerts a dominant influence on both the grinding force and recurrence metrics. However, for specific indicators, the feed rate emerges as the prevailing factor. The study also demonstrates a strong correlation between cutting parameters and the harmonic content of the grinding force signal. Furthermore, increasing cutting speed was found to generally stabilize the process, whereas variations in feed rate may either enhance or deteriorate machining stability.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Department of Applied Mechanics, Faculty of Mechanical Engineering, Lublin University of Technology, 20-618 Lublin, Poland.
Department of Manufacturing Techniques and Automation, Faculty of Mechanical Engineering and Aeronautics, Rzeszow University of Technology, 35-959 Rzeszów, Poland.

Grant.Agency: This research was funded by the Lublin University of Technology, Poland, under re- 595 search grant no. FD-20/IM-5/053 (KK), and PODKARPACKIE CENTRUM INNOWACJI, grant 596 number 50/PRZ/1/DG/PCI/2020, grant title: Innovative manufacturing technology of cut

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: The authors declare no conflicts of interest.
